prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "站立提踵" (standing calf raise) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of a standing calf raise: standing fully upright with both feet on the ground hip-width apart, weight shifted onto the balls of the feet, both heels lifted as high as possible (full plantarflexion of the ankles), legs straight and aligned; torso upright with neutral spine, arms hanging at the sides or hands on hips; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: tibialis posterior and small intrinsic foot muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腓肠肌" pointing to the gastrocnemius (upper calf); "比目鱼肌" pointing to the soleus (deep calf); "足部稳定肌" pointing to the foot stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
